characters.name: Ashley Schaeffer
characters.description: Ashley SchaefferNickname(s)AshTitlesJournalist at Channel 10 NewsPolitical Journalist atCity HallProfessionJournalismPersonalRomance(s)Mike Flaherty(ex-boyfriend)BackstagePortrayed byCarla GuginoSeasons1•3First seenLast seenPrototypeIt Happened One NightEpisodes13Ashley Schaefferis a principal character inSeason 1ofSpin City, portrayed byCarla Gugino. Ashley works as a political journalist atCity Hall. She is smart, witty and oftentimes a headache for hapless Press SecretaryPaul Lassiteras he folds easily under her scrutiny of the administration. She and deputy mayorMike Flahertybanter and spar, which eventually causes their mutual attraction, resulting in a romance. They move in together at the start of the series, until Ashley is offered a job in Los Angeles and leaves New York. Ashley works for an unnamed newspaper coveringCity Hall, specifically theWinston Administration. Despite the obvious conflict of interest, she and Mike are in a longtime committed relationship. While both are very capable at their jobs, Ashley sometimes questions whether she lets Mike off the hook too easily, specifically when Mike makes a crack suggesting as much in an interview. Ashley reacts by successfully challenging the administration's flip-flopping stance on help to AIDS victims.[1]At one point, Ashley is offered the chance to anchor news on television, but realises she suffers from stage fright on camera and bombs at her audition. They offer her a callback, however, and she is hired as a reporter for Channel 10 News.[2]Though excited to move into a different area of her work, Ashley soon finds that she's just covering crazy human interest stories around the city. She tries to expand her role to her previous area of politics by sniffing out any possibly scoops. When Carter decides to to protest the ban on gay marriage, Ashley jumps at the chance for an exclusive on the story and gets it. She directs a camera man during the ceremony and interviews Carter afterwards.[3] Ashley works as a political journalist atCity Hall. She is smart, witty and oftentimes a headache for hapless Press SecretaryPaul Lassiteras he folds easily under her scrutiny of the administration. She and deputy mayorMike Flahertybanter and spar, which eventually causes their mutual attraction, resulting in a romance. They move in together at the start of the series, until Ashley is offered a job in Los Angeles and leaves New York.

characters.name: Becky Corrigan
characters.description: Becky CorriganBornRebecca CorriganFromWisconsinPersonalRomance(s)James HobertBackstagePortrayed byJennifer GarnerSeasonsSeason 1Only EpisodeThe CompetitionBecky Corriganis a character inSeason 1ofSpin City, portrayed by Jennifer Garner. Becky is a childhood friend of James. They grew up in the same town and their families know each other. James and Becky start dating in high school and James, to the surprise of everyone, is actually in some form of long-distance relationship with Becky. Despite this, James has flings during the time they are supposedly together, like with Gwen, the reporter doing a feature on Mike for Manhattan Magazine. It turns out it's because James wants to break up with Becky and can't get up the nerve to do so, as they've been together for 7 years and he doesn't want to hurt her. When she surprises him with a visit, he scrambles to get the courage to tell her the truth. Becky's upset as her cat recently died and the cats her mother and sister got to cheer her up, didn't get along and one of them killed the other. This has James hesitating to broach the subject. Becky also wants to see the city, but James doesn't want to lead her on. When he finally does, break up with Becky, it's at the start of their 14-hour bus trip back to Wisconsin for Thanksgiving.

characters.name: Caitlin Moore
characters.description: Caitlin MooreProfessionPublic RelationsPersonalRomance(s)Charlie CrawfordBackstagePortrayed byHeather LocklearSeasons4•5•6First seenLast seenCatcher in the BronxA Friend in NeedEpisodes71Caitlin Mooreis a principal character onSpin City, portrayed byHeather Locklear. Caitlin Mooreis the Deputy Mayor’s Chief of Staff onSpin City, introduced in Season 4 as a driven, polished political strategist with a talent for crisis management and a tendency to micromanage everyone around her. Intelligent, ambitious, and occasionally neurotic, she quickly becomes one of the Mayor’s most valuable advisors — and one ofCharlie Crawford'smost complicated relationships. Caitlin Moorearrives atCity HallinSeason 4, stepping into the role of Chief of Staff after a successful career in political consulting. Known for her sharp instincts and polished professionalism, she brings a more structured, disciplined energy to the Mayor’s office, often clashing with the more chaotic personalities around her. Despite her perfectionist tendencies, Caitlin is deeply committed to public service and takes pride in shaping policy, managing crises, and keeping the administration running smoothly.

characters.name: Carter Heywood
characters.description:
Carter HeywoodBornCarter Sebastian Heywood[1]TitlesSpecial Assistant on Minority Affairs atCity HallActivistProfessionPoliticsPersonalFamilyRagsRomance(s)Spence Kaymer(ex-boyfriend)Mike Flaherty(one-sided crush)BackstagePortrayed byMichael BoatmanFirst seenPrototypeEpisodes145Carter Heywoodis a principal character onSpin City, portrayed byMichael Boatman. Carter works as the head of minority affairs atCity Hall.
When the mayor responds to the question of whether he will participate in the Gay Pride Parade with "What are you? Drunk?",Mikedecides they need a gay colleague. He then sees Carter, a gay rights activist, commenting on the mayor on television and decides to hire him.[2]Carter promises he will be the mayor's worst nightmare. Carter gets settled in and becomes good friends with everyone, especially with Stuart, who later becomes his roommate. Carter works as the head of minority affairs atCity Hall.
When the mayor responds to the question of whether he will participate in the Gay Pride Parade with "What are you? Drunk?",Mikedecides they need a gay colleague. He then sees Carter, a gay rights activist, commenting on the mayor on television and decides to hire him.[2]Carter promises he will be the mayor's worst nightmare. Carter gets settled in and becomes good friends with everyone, especially with Stuart, who later becomes his roommate.

characters.name: Howard Gould
characters.description: Howard GouldTitlesPolice Commissioner ofNew York CityProfessionLaw EnforcementBackstagePortrayed byJerry AdlerSeasonsSeason 1Only EpisodeDog Day AfternoonHoward Gouldis a character inSeason 1ofSpin City, portrayed byJerry Adler. Gould criticises theWinston Administrationfor taking political credit for the decrease in crime, when he considers it the result of the city's police force's efforts. Gould lets this be known at a press conference atCity Hallon the matter, andMayor Winstonchastises Gould for it afterwards, arguing that his administration is responsible for the capacity of the force and stating that he fights theCity Councilon the matter, who would rather see the force decreased. Winston infers that he would like to replace Gould should the latter not ease up on the administration. Gould discusses it withMike Flaherty, who tries to smoothe things over unsuccesfully. Gould insists that the Mayor's alledged support for the police force is empty, as the mayor doesn't attend any functions to show his support. Mike decides to honour the most decorated, recently deceased canine officer,Bingo, with a funeral which the mayor will attend and speak at. Gould is happy with the effort and thanks the mayor.

characters.name: James Hobert
characters.description: James HobertBornJames Leonard Hobert III[1]Birthday1972[2]Nickname(s)Scooter(byStuart)FromWisconsinTitlesSpeechwriter atWinston AdministrationEducationColumbia UniversityProfessionPoliticsPersonalFamilyAunt Sarah †(family cow)Ms. Hobert(sister)Mr. Hobert(unnamed grandfather)Romance(s)Marie Paterno(fling)Meg Winston(fling)Katie(dated)Nikki Faber(unrequited crush)Becky Corrigan(ex-girlfriend)Gwen(fling)BackstagePortrayed byAlexander ChaplinSeasons1•2•3•4First seenLast seenPrototypeGoodbye: Part 2Episodes100James Hobertis a principal character onSpin City, portrayed byAlexander Chaplin. James works as themayor'sspeech writer. He is a naive, jovial, attentive, mature, educated, selfless, honorable, optimistic, bold, encouraging, respectful, thoughtful, judicious, aspiring, meticulous, even-tempered, sensitive, humble, observant, brilliant, empathetic, rational and trusting young man who can be very charming because of his innocence. He was born in Wisconsin, where he was a Pig Contest winner. He likes to shoutWisconsin rules!from time to time. During the fourth season he was demoted from speech writer to Mike's assistant. Mike disguises this demotion by considering him the Deputy Deputy Mayor. After season four, Chaplin, along with the Britton and Dillard, left due to the shows move from New York City to Los Angeles. James works as themayor'sspeech writer. He is a naive, jovial, attentive, mature, educated, selfless, honorable, optimistic, bold, encouraging, respectful, thoughtful, judicious, aspiring, meticulous, even-tempered, sensitive, humble, observant, brilliant, empathetic, rational and trusting young man who can be very charming because of his innocence. He was born in Wisconsin, where he was a Pig Contest winner. He likes to shoutWisconsin rules!from time to time.

characters.name: Janelle Cooper
characters.description: Janelle CooperTitlesSecretary to the Mayor of New YorkSecretary to the Deputy MayorPersonalRomance(s)Randall WinstonBackstagePortrayed byVictoria DillardSeasons1•2•3•4First seenLast seenPrototypeGoodbye: Part 2Episodes91Janelle Cooperis a principal character onSpin City, portrayed byVictoria Dillard. Janelle is a smart and tough woman, who doesn't take crap from anyone. She works as the secretary to the Deputy Mayor ofNew York City, and is promoted to secretary to the mayor after asking for a raise. Janelle is quick-witted and seems to enjoy her work withMike Flaherty, keeping him appraised of relevant developments. She is friends with Nikki, with whom she shares an understanding and, at times, exasperation at their male-dominated work environment. She and Stacey also become friendly when Stacey joins the office. Janelle is generally respected around the office and even Stuart doesn't dare his usual antics with her. At one point, Janelle starts dating the mayor. Janelle appears in every episode in seasons 2-4, and in 91 episodes total. Janelle is a smart and tough woman, who doesn't take crap from anyone. She and Stacey also become friendly when Stacey joins the office. Janelle is generally respected around the office and even Stuart doesn't dare his usual antics with her. At one point, Janelle starts dating the mayor.

She and Stacey also become friendly when Stacey joins the office. Janelle is generally respected around the office and even Stuart doesn't dare his usual antics with her. At one point, Janelle starts dating the mayor. Janelle appears in every episode in seasons 2-4, and in 91 episodes total. Janelle is a smart and tough woman, who doesn't take crap from anyone. She works as the secretary to the Deputy Mayor ofNew York City, and is promoted to secretary to the mayor after asking for a raise. Janelle is quick-witted and seems to enjoy her work withMike Flaherty, keeping him appraised of relevant developments. She and Stacey also become friendly when Stacey joins the office. Janelle is generally respected around the office and even Stuart doesn't dare his usual antics with her. At one point, Janelle starts dating the mayor.

characters.name: Karen Palmieri
characters.description: Karen PalmieriTitlesStaff atWinston AdministrationIntern atDeputy Mayor's OfficeProfessionPoliticsPersonalRomance(s)James Hobert(unrequited crush)BackstagePortrayed byTaylor StanleySeasonsSeason 1First seenLast seenPrototypeGabby's SongEpisodes8 Karen Palmieriis a recurring character inSeason 1ofSpin City,portrayed byTaylor Stanley. Karen is a sweet, naive and bubbly college intern with theWinston Administrationfor a semester.Miketries to show her the ropes and introduce her to the harsh reality of politics, but Karen's wide-eyed optimism usually foils those efforts. She's always in a good mood and tends to abandon a situation if it brings her down. For instance when she andPaulattend a work function together and he's in a bad mood, she complains to Mike and he lets her 'off the hook' as the office all paired off to avoid chaperoningMrs. Winston.

characters.name: Laurie Parres
characters.description: Laurie ParresTitlesAttorneyEducationWellesley CollegeProfessionLawPersonalFamilyCarol Parres(mother)Jenny Parres(sister)Romance(s)Mike FlahertyBackstagePortrayed byPaula MarshallSeasonsSeason 2First seenLast seenPaul Flew Over The Cuckoo's NestIt Happened One NightEpisodes7Laurie Parresis a recurring character onSpin City, portrayed byPaula Marshall. Laurie is an accomplished, together woman who knows what she wants in life. She is smart and witty. She works as a divorce attorney in New York and is hired byHelen Winstonwhen the latter divorces Mayor Winston. Laurie squares off withMike Flahertywho assumes the position as the mayor's council once he meets Laurie and they soon start dating. She also become good friends withNikki Faber. Laurie meets theWinston Administrationwhen she acts asHelen Winston's divorce attorney. Mike immediately becomes interested in Laurie, only taking up the position of legal council to the mayor in order to spend time with Laurie. Laurie enjoys sparring with Mike and they eventually start flirting while going over proceedings. Mike asks out Laurie, but she rejects him as she has a rule of not dating opposing council. At a dinner to discuss parameters of the case, Mike tries to make it romantic, which annoys Laurie as she explicity said she didn't want to date during the case. She relays that she's been taking it easy on him because he's rusty when it comes to practicing law and, despite the mayor not wanting much in the divorce, she now takes everything. Mike brushes up on litigation and counters, leading to a standoff between them. They both relent, settle the case and start dating.[1] Laurie is an accomplished, together woman who knows what she wants in life. She is smart and witty. She works as a divorce attorney in New York and is hired byHelen Winstonwhen the latter divorces Mayor Winston. Laurie squares off withMike Flahertywho assumes the position as the mayor's council once he meets Laurie and they soon start dating. She also become good friends withNikki Faber.

characters.name: Marie Paterno
characters.description: Marie PaternoPersonalFamilyStacey Paterno(niece)Romance(s)James Hobert(fling)BackstagePortrayed byPriscilla PresleySeasonsSeason 3First seenDick Clark's Rockin' Make-Out Party '99Marie Paternois a character inSeason 3ofSpin City, portrayed by Priscilla Presley.

characters.name: Meg Winston
characters.description: Meg WinstonBornMegan WinstonFromNew York CityEducationStudent at BerkeleyPersonalFamilyRandall Winston(father)Helen Winston(mother)Randall Winston Sr.(paternal grandfather)Romance(s)James Hobert(fling)BackstagePortrayed byAlyssa MilanoSeasonsSeason 2First seenLast seenThey Shoot Horses, Don't They?Rain on My CharadesEpisodes2Meg Winstonis a character inSeason 2ofSpin City, portrayed by Alyssa Milano. Meg is the mayor's college-aged daughter who attends Berkeley. She is free-spirited, bold and rebellious, in stark contrast to her conservative parents. Her parents are also very old-fashioned, leading Meg to cry out for her father's attention with a protest against the conditions for the horses drawing the carriages in Central Park. Eventually, the mayor understands and starts spending some time with his daughter. Mike is the one to mend fences and talk to Meg about the protest, as it poses a political issue for theWinston Administration. While atCity Hall, Meg becomes interested inJamesand they have a fling, much to the mayor's dispair.

characters.name: Mike Flaherty
characters.description: Mike FlahertyBornMichael Patrick FlahertyBirthdayDecember/January[1]1967[2]Nickname(s)Mike • MikeyFromNew York City, NYTitlesDeputy Mayor ofNew York CityEducationFordham UniversityProfessionPoliticsPersonalFamilyMacy Flaherty(mother)Romance(s)Nikki Faber(ex-girlfriend)Heidi Klum(dated)Laurie Parres(dated)Ashley Schaeffer(ex-girlfriend)Renee Miller(dated)Caitlin Moore(ex-girlfriend)BackstagePortrayed byMichael J. FoxSeasons1•2•3•4•6First seenLast seenPrototypeWife With MikeyEpisodes103Michael Flahertyis the protagonist ofSpin City. He is portrayed byMichael J. Fox. Mike is a quick-witted, smart aleck political strategist who lives for his job as Deputy Mayor ofNew York City. He grew up in the city as an only child raised by a single mother,Macy Flaherty,[1]where he attended Stuyvesant High School and later Fordham University.[3]Although Mike often uses humour and disarmament to mask it he has a very big heart and treats his staff more like family than employees. His drive to succeed often clashes with the romantic side of his personal life. Mike work as the campaign manager forRandall Winstonin the latter's first mayoral run and, following their success, is hired by Winston as the Deputy Mayor. While Winston is the typical rich white male politician, who loves and thrives in the spotlight, he isn't that interested in playing the game, even frequently causing gaffes that Mike works around the clock to clean up. Another facet of Mike's political career becomes the increasingly tenuous relationship between the Mayor and his wife, which Mike does everything to sustain in order to avoid scandal. Despite his efforts, the Mayor does eventually divorce to little political fanfare as it doesn't impact public opinion of the administration. Mike is a quick-witted, smart aleck political strategist who lives for his job as Deputy Mayor ofNew York City. He grew up in the city as an only child raised by a single mother,Macy Flaherty,[1]where he attended Stuyvesant High School and later Fordham University.[3]Although Mike often uses humour and disarmament to mask it he has a very big heart and treats his staff more like family than employees. His drive to succeed often clashes with the romantic side of his personal life.

characters.name: Nikki Faber
characters.description: Nikki FaberBornNicole Katherine Faber[1]Birthday20 July 1968[2]Nickname(s)Nikki • NikFromBoston, MATitlesAccountant atWinston AdministrationEducationVassar[3]ProfessionPoliticsPersonalRomance(s)Mike FlahertyArthurJay(dated)BackstagePortrayed byConnie BrittonSeasons1•2•3•4First seenLast seenPrototypeGoodbye: Part 2Episodes100Nikki Faberis a principal character onSpin City, portrayed byConnie Britton. Nikki is a smart, sweet and witty woman. She works with accounting for theWinston Administration, despite having aimed to avoid that field, stating she would find it boring. However, her job entails a lot of political maneuvering and networking, which is outside the purview of a regular accounting position, which Nikki enjoys. She works in an almost all-male office, and keeps the guys in check when necessary. Nikki forms a friendship and strong alliance with the other woman in the office,Janelle, and later when Janelle works for the mayor, Nikki and Janelle become friends withMike'sfiery new secretary,Stacey. Generally, Nikki gets along well with everyone, and also forms a close friendship with Carter when he joins the administration. Nikki and Mike have a good friendship from the off and later develop feelings for each other. Nikki was born in Boston, Massachusetts somewhere in the late sixties and relocated to New York at age thirteen. She was selected by Randall sometime after he became theMayorof New York City. She enjoys her job, but mostly focuses on her personal life, specifically her desire to get married and have children, finding it difficult to meet someone in the city that's not completely insane. Because she's so interested in finding a relationship, Nikki often dates moochers who don't appreciate her, although it usually never lasts long. WhenCarteris hired, they quickly hit it off and develop a sweet friendship, often teasing each other. Nikki helps Carter with his protest against the ban on gay marriage, where they will demonstrate how easy it is for them to get married in a hetero ceremony despite not being in love. The protest is a success as it generates the media attention Carter hoped for, despite them not going through with the nuptials as it was affecting Nikki emotionally. Despite her willingness to go through with it, he didn't want her to feel bad and also thought them getting to the altar with a priest presiding over a ceremony enough to prove the point.[4]When Carter later dates famous fashion designer Isaac Mizrahi, Nikki gets a chance to model one of Mizrahi's designs, but Mike's damage control of the mayor's failing marriage sees him accidentally knock her over on the runway.[5] Nikki is a smart, sweet and witty woman. She works with accounting for theWinston Administration, despite having aimed to avoid that field, stating she would find it boring. However, her job entails a lot of political maneuvering and networking, which is outside the purview of a regular accounting position, which Nikki enjoys. She works in an alm

characters.name: Paul Lassiter
characters.description: Paul LassiterBornPaul Thomas Lassiter[1]Birthday1961[2]Nickname(s)PaulieBig Daddy(by himself; Mike under duress)Captain Good News(by himself)FromQueens, NYTitlesPress Secretary atCity HallEducationNotre DameSt. Mary's SchoolProfessionPoliticsPersonalFamilyAbby LassiterRomance(s)Claudia SacksBackstagePortrayed byRichard KindFirst seenLast seenPrototypeA Friend in NeedEpisodes145 Paul Lassiteris a principal character onSpin City, portrayed byRichard Kind. Paul works as the mayor's press secretary. He's a very gullible man and a cheapskate, but he has a good heart. And he is also known as protective, accountable, understanding, loyal, low-key, attentive, selfless, sensitive, independent, trustworthy, educated, righteous, patient, affectionate, upbeat, loving, level-headed, artistic, sweet-tempered, sensible, industrious, tidy, emotional and reasonable. He is not too bright and comes up, from time to time, with stupid ideas, mostly to save money. Paul works as the mayor's press secretary. characters.name: Rags
characters.description: RagsBornReginald R. Ragamuffin III[1]PersonalFamilyCarter HeywoodBackstagePortrayed byTim AllenRegis PhilbinSeasons3•4•5•6First seenDead Dog TalkingRagsis an animal onSpin City, voiced by Tim Allen and Regis Philbin.

characters.name: Randall Winston
characters.description: Randall WinstonBirthday1947[1]FromConnecticutTitlesMayor ofNew York CityEducationYale UniversityProfessionPoliticsPersonalFamilyMeg Winston(daughter)Romance(s)Janelle Cooper(dated)Candice Foxworthy(dated)Jennifer(fling)Helen Winston(ex-wife)Holly Cohen(emotional affair)BackstagePortrayed byBarry BostwickFirst seenLast seenPrototypeA Friend in NeedEpisodes144Randall Winstonis a principal character onSpin City, portrayed byBarry Bostwick. Randall Winston works as the Mayor ofNew York City. Mayor Winston works as a city councilman for ten years prior to becoming Mayor and also a borough President for some time. He is a Democrat (this fact is mentioned only in passing, such as brief references to the DNC). He is a liberal and believes strongly in public education. It is implied, though not stated directly, that Winston ran for mayor at the instruction/coercion ofMike Flaherty, who became his Deputy Mayor for most of his term. Winston appears to be an intelligent, mature, accountable, understanding, optimistic, righteous, well-mannered, industrious, no-nonsense, sensible, trustworthy, observant, noble, meticulous, assertive, selfless, open-minded, reasonable, warm-hearted, independent, nice, sophisitcated, tidy, outspoken, nurturing and charismatic mayor who has the ability to connect with voters. Much of this is a carefully orchestrated sham by his staff, especially Mike. The Mayor is actually unbalanced in a number of ways. He is childish, vain, outrageously dim and often oblivious to events, such as forgetting he is the mayor. He behaves in very odd ways behind the scenes, and sometimes in front of them. He has little or no self-control, blurts things out (when asked if he would march in the Gay Pride Parade, he replied "What are you? Drunk?") and generally behaves like a child. He is not, however, without a degree of media skill - he is capable of thinking on his feet and 'winging it' at public events when required. The Mayor also suffers from a lack of focus, letting his mind wander from topic to topic almost at random, and often becoming obsessive and carried away with minor, trivial things. characters.name: Randall Winston
characters.description: Randall WinstonBirthday1947[1]FromConnecticutTitlesMayor ofNew York CityEducationYale UniversityProfessionPoliticsPersonalFamilyMeg Winston(daughter)Romance(s)Janelle Cooper(dated)Candice Foxworthy(dated)Jennifer(fling)Helen Winston(ex-wife)Holly Cohen(emotional affair)BackstagePortrayed byBarry BostwickFirst seenLast seenPrototypeA Friend in NeedEpisodes144Randall Winstonis a principal character onSpin City, portrayed byBarry Bostwick. Randall Winston works as the Mayor ofNew York City. Mayor Winston works as a city councilman for ten years prior to becoming Mayor and also a borough President for some time. He is a Democrat (this fact is mentioned only in passing, such as brief references to the DNC). He is a liberal and believes strongly in public education. It is implied, though not stated directly, that Winston ran for mayor at the instruction/coercion ofMike Flaherty, who became his Deputy Mayor for most of his term. Winston appears to be an intelligent, mature, accountable, understanding, optimistic, righteous, well-mannered, industrious, no-nonsense, sensible, trustworthy, observant, noble, meticulous, assertive, selfless, open-minded, reasonable, warm-hearted, independent, nice, sophisitcated, tidy, outspoken, nurturing and charismatic mayor who has the ability to connect with voters. Much of this is a carefully orchestrated sham by his staff, especially Mike. The Mayor is actually unbalanced in a number of ways. He is childish, vain, outrageously dim and often oblivious to events, such as forgetting he is the mayor. He behaves in very odd ways behind the scenes, and sometimes in front of them. He has little or no self-control, blurts things out (when asked if he would march in the Gay Pride Parade, he replied "What are you? Drunk?") and generally behaves like a child. He is not, however, without a degree of media skill - he is capable of thinking on his feet and 'winging it' at public events when required. The Mayor also suffers from a lack of focus, letting his mind wander from topic to topic almost at random, and often becoming obsessive and carried away with minor, trivial things.
